title: Xerochrysum macranthum
taxonomy: Xerochrysum macranthum is a flowering plant, specifically classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Xerochrysum, which is native to Australia. Xerochrysum macranthum is further classified as a member of the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. This plant is native to eastern New South Wales and southern Queensland, with its common name being the yellow button or strawflower. Xerochrysum macranthum is an annual herb that grows up to 30 centimeters in height, producing bright yellow daisy-like flowers.
uses: Xerochrysum macranthum, also known as the strawflower, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. Traditional healers may also use the plant to soothe minor wounds and reduce inflammation.

The main active constituents of the plant include borneol, bornyl acetate, and β-pinene. These compounds contribute to the plant's antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. intro: Xerochrysum macranthum, also known as Yellow Button or Golden Everlasting, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Australia. This plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Xerochrysum macranthum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Xerochrysum macranthum include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
